perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, " Sensitive Information" s hall include all information or material that has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged might lead to the security of the Disclosing Party’s physical or information assets or the safety of its staff and customers being compromised . If Sensitive Information is in a physical or electronic form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word s OFFICIAL -SENSITIVE , or some similar warning , in line with HMG’s protective marking scheme. If Sensitive Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Sensitive Information.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, " Sensitive Information" shall include all information or material that might lead to the security of the Disclosing Party’s physical or information assets or the safety of its staff and customers being compromised. If Sensitive Information is in a physical or electronic form, the Disclosing Party shall label or stamp the materials with the word s OFFICIAL -SENSITIVE , or some similar warning , in line with HMG’s protective marking scheme. If Sensitive Information is transmitted orally, the Disclosing Party shall promptly provide a writing indicating that such oral communication constituted Sensitive Information.
perturbation.explanation: The clause "has or could have commercial value or other utility in the business in which Disclosing Party is engaged" has been removed from the definition of 'Sensitive Information'. This creates an in-text contradiction, because the term 'Sensitive Information' is referenced in Obligations of Receiving Party, which is now ambiguous regarding the scope of confidential information, specifically concerning commercially valuable information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Sensitive Information' to include information with commercial value. The changed text omits this aspect from the definition. The contradicted text imposes strict obligations on the Receiving Party regarding 'Sensitive Information'. By removing 'commercial value' from the definition, the scope of 'Sensitive Information' becomes ambiguous, particularly with regard to commercially valuable information, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about the Receiving Party's obligations to protect that commercially valuable information.

perturbation.original_text: Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information.
perturbation.explanation: The clause "Recei ving Party shall put in place effective governance controls to monitor compliance and respond to (and report) any security breach incidents." was removed. This weakens the 'Obligations of Receiving Party', creating an in-text contradiction because other clauses in the same section assume robust governance controls are in place. If there is no governance controls in place to detect incidents, it makes the other clauses in the section, harder to enforce.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Recei ving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the requirement for the Receiving Party to put in place effective governance controls creates a strong contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text describes obligations that are dependent on the Receiving Party actively restricting access to sensitive information and maintaining its confidentiality. Without the governance controls, it becomes significantly more difficult, if not impossible, to enforce these obligations effectively. Therefore, the omission directly undermines the ability of the Receiving Party to meet its responsibilities as outlined in the contradicted section, creating a clear in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Removing "contractors" from the clause "Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement." creates an in-text contradiction. Section 3 has removed contractors, meaning the Receiving party only has to restrict the access of Sensitive Information to employees and third parties, therefore if contractors don't sign a non-disclosure agreement they can disclose that information freely without consequence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party must restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees, contractors, and third parties. The changed text removes 'contractors' from this list, meaning the Receiving Party is no longer explicitly obligated to restrict contractor access. The contradicted text states the Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees and third parties. The removal of 'contractors' in the changed text creates a contradiction because contractors are not explicitly required to sign nondisclosure agreements, which could potentially cause sensitive information to be leaked by the contractors, resulting in an inconsistency and contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information within a reasonable time period if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "within a reasonable time period" has been removed. This creates an in-text contradiction, as the Receiving Party is obligated to return materials but without a clear timeline, enforcement is weakened. This contradicts the intention of section 3 since without that phrase the return of documents with 'sensitive information' might not occur within a timely manner, creating an enforcement issue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party shall hold and maintain the Sensitive Information in strictest confidence for the sole and exclusive benefit of the Disclosing Party. Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to Sensitive Information to employees and third parties as is reasonably required and shall require those persons to sign nondisclosure restrictions at least as protective as those in this Agreement. Receiving Party shall not, without prior written approval of Disclosing Party, use for Receiving Party's own benefi t, publish, copy, or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of Disclosing Party, any Sensitive Information. Receiving Party shall return to Disclosing Party any and all records, notes, and other writ ten, printed, or tangible materials in its possession pertaining to Sensitive Information if Disclosing Party requests it in writing.
perturbation.justification: The removal of "within a reasonable time period" from the original text creates a contradiction with the intention of the contradicted text. The contradicted text emphasizes maintaining sensitive information and restricting access. Without a specified timeframe for returning the information, as highlighted in the explanation, the Receiving Party could delay the return indefinitely, thus contradicting the purpose of maintaining confidentiality and potentially harming the Disclosing Party. This aligns with the Omissions – In Text Contradiction definition, as essential language regarding the timeframe has been removed, weakening enforcement.
